failure_modes.text: Availability-style substitution can force a novel uncertainty problem into a familiar frame that does not actually fit.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test frame fit before applying a familiar uncertainty model: check whether the current situation has the same structure as the template, and switch tools when it does not.

failure_modes.text: Overconfidence can turn fluency with the uncertainty language into an illusion of knowing.
failure_modes.mitigation: Calibrate confidence against what you actually know: when uncertainty language flows easily, verify that the fluency reflects genuine understanding, not just comfort with the vocabulary.

failure_modes.text: Conjunctive optimism can make a long chain of dependencies look sturdier than it really is.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test conjunctive plans: when a chain of dependencies must all succeed, multiply the independent failure probabilities to see the compound risk — it is almost always worse than intuition suggests.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What You Have to Believe Analysis

premortem_questions.text: If this decision were to fail a year from now, what assumptions would have proven wrong?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this decision were to fail a year from now, what assumptions would have proven wrong?

premortem_questions.text: Does this problem structure truly match the framework I am applying, or am I torturing reality so that it fits my model?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Does this problem structure truly match the framework I am applying, or am I torturing reality so that it fits my model?

heuristics.text: Use a knowns-and-unknowns map to separate what is already visible from what still needs de-risking or discovery.
Build several plausible scenarios instead of one forecast when the range of outcomes matters more than precision theater.
Generate an active hypothesis early so ambiguity does not become a license for endless unguided data gathering.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the intervention layer on explicit unknown mapping, scenario work, and anti-overconfidence checks rather than broad strategic ambiguity talk.
donor_drops: Did not preserve generic uncertainty philosophy because the raw source is strongest on multi-path action rules and anti-substitution discipline.
open_questions: The source is rich on deliberate-then-act sequencing, but the chunk set stays on the most reusable intervention surface.

allies: The raw source is rich on scenario analysis, hypothesis-driven work, and latticework thinking.
antagonists: The source explicitly names commitment-bias, representativeness-bias, and confirmation-bias pressure.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against commitment-bias, representativeness-bias, and confirmation-bias.
